_README: Per-species image strategy used by scripts/refresh-images.ts. For each species we specify a list of {lifeStage, sex, count} buckets. The script will query iNaturalist with the corresponding filters, take the top N photos per bucket, and persist into SpeciesImage. To pin a specific photo (e.g. the one we KNOW is the best juvenile catshark image), add it to `overrides` as a SpeciesImage row — those are upserted with curated=true and never overwritten by the script.

Pollachius pollachius._note: Juveniles look very different (golden lateral stripe in kelp); show both.

Labrus bergylta._note: Plain green/brown vs red-with-white-spots morph; juveniles emerald-green.

Labrus mixtus._note: Sexual dimorphism is extreme: ♂ blue/orange, ♀ pink with 3 dorsal blotches.

Callionymus lyra._note: ♂ elongated dorsal ray + blue stripes; ♀ cryptic brown saddles.

Pleuronectes platessa._note: Add larva/post-settlement if iNat has it; flatfish pre-eye-migration is unrecognisable.

Scyliorhinus canicula._note: Egg case is itself an ID target — annotated as 'egg' on iNat.

Gadus morhua._note: Juveniles reddish-brown camouflage in kelp; adults mottled olive/silver.

Dicentrarchus labrax._note: Juveniles have distinct black spots; adults plain silver.

overrides._README: Add specific photo overrides here. Shape: { scientificName: [{ url, attribution, sourceUrl, license, lifeStage, sex, ordering }, ...] }. These are upserted with curated=true on every script run and never auto-deleted. Real overrides go at the top level of this object; any key starting with an underscore is ignored by the script.
